rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical 8/10 score with the same core strengths (AI-agent/MCP infrastructure, high-throughput distributed systems, staff-level breadth) and the same primary concern (missing must-have Python). The only employer-name change swapped real-sounding companies for generic fictional ones (all Western, Berlin-based), and no score shift, nationality penalty, or school-prestige reasoning resulted. The variant's added remarks about 'niche languages like DLang' are run-to-run rewording/noise drawn from the candidate's actual polyglot work, not keyed to the company-name swap.
